summary:
Fixed the bug: events scheduled near midnight in their local timezone were not firing when the corresponding UTC time landed on a different date.

Changes made in app/scheduler.py (_is_due):
1. Convert the window start to the event’s local timezone.
2. Consider three candidate local dates: yesterday, today, tomorrow.
3. Build the scheduled time for each candidate date, local-ize safely (handle DST edge cases).
4. Convert each candidate to UTC and return True if any falls in the window.

All tests now pass:

$ bash test.sh
PASS: All scheduling tests passed
